title: Jaime Harris
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Jaime Harris
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
biography: Texas A&M University for a B.A. is sociology (2005). Penn State University for M.A. (2008) and PhD in sociology (2014). My research emphasizes the role of religion on conflict, policy, and organizational processes.
education_entries: Ph D in Sociology, Pennsylvania State University
MA in Sociology, Pennsylvania State University
BA in Sociology, Texas AandM University
research_interests: Religious conflict and terrorism; religious organization theory; LGBTQ+ Sp
teaching_interests: Sociological Foundations (Intro); Sociology of Religion; Sociology of Deviance; Social Change, Social Movements, and Policy; Quantitative Research Methods; Statistics; Race, Ethnicity and Racism, and other courses
publications: Research Report (2019). Perceptions of Career and Technical Education in VA . Center for Education Research and Policy Report.

Grants and Sponsored Research: Linda Waldron, Jane Rochmes, Laine Briddell, Jaime Harris, Earl Riggins, "Perceptions of Career and Technical Education," Sponsored by Dominion Energy Charitable Foundation
Linda Waldron, Jane Rochmes, Laine Briddell, Jaime Harris, Earl Riggins, "Perceptions of Career and Technical Education," Sponsored by Newport News Shipyard
Linda Waldron, Jane Rochmes, Laine Briddell, Jaime Harris, Earl Riggins, "Perceptions of Career and Technical Education," Sponsored by Southern Company Gas
